name: Macromolecule

phase_0.boundary_condition: The solvent excluded volume and thermodynamic envelope defining the macromolecular surface.

VINIT.justification: Represents the initial state of free monomers in solution before any covalent or noncovalent assembly begins.

TANCH.justification: Defines the closed physical boundary of the macromolecule separating its internal coordinates from the bulk solvent.

AFWD.justification: The forward morphism of adding a monomer to the growing polymer chain via condensation reaction.

AREV.justification: The reverse morphism of breaking a covalent bond in the polymer backbone through the addition of water.

CLINK.justification: The composition of multiple elongation steps chaining together to form the primary sequence.

IMSCRIB.justification: The identity operation where the folded macromolecule recognizes and maintains its own conformational energy minimum.

FSPLIT.justification: The comultiplication where a multimeric complex branches into its distinct constituent monomeric subunits.

FFUSE.justification: The multiplication where the distinct constituent monomeric subunits reconstitute the original multimeric complex.

EVALT.justification: The affirmative state where the macromolecule achieves its correct functional three dimensional structure.

EVALF.justification: The negative state where the macromolecule forms nonfunctional amorphous or amyloid aggregates.

ENGAGR.justification: The paradice state where the macromolecule simultaneously possesses secondary structure elements and a fluctuating tertiary core.

IFIX.justification: The permanent fixation of the tertiary structure through irreversible covalent bonds between cysteine residues.

phase_3.void_description: The unactivated monomer pool existing as independent entities in the bulk solvent without any polymeric or folded structure.
phase_3.true_description: The native functional conformation where all structural domains are correctly folded and thermodynamically stable.
phase_3.false_description: The misfolded or aggregated state where hydrophobic residues are exposed and the molecule lacks biological activity.
phase_3.both_description: The molten globule intermediate state exhibiting both defined secondary structure and highly mobile unstructured tertiary fluctuations.

steps.domain_action: Initialize the unactivated monomer pool in the aqueous solvent.
Activate the monomer by attaching a high energy leaving group.
Chain the activated monomer to the growing polymer backbone.
Propagate the polymer chain through sequential monomer addition.
Complete the primary sequence synthesis of the linear macromolecule.
Dissociate the multimeric complex into Monomeric subunit A and Monomeric subunit B.
Fold Monomeric subunit A into the native functional conformation.
Expose the binding interface of Monomeric subunit A to the solvent.
Misfold Monomeric subunit B into an aggregated nonfunctional state.
Bury the hydrophobic core of Monomeric subunit B via reverse conformational collapse.
Reassociate Monomeric subunit A and Monomeric subunit B to reconstitute the multimeric protein complex.
Suspend the reconstituted complex in the molten globule phase holding both structured and unstructured characteristics.
Lock the quaternary structure via irreversible disulfide crosslinking.
Recognize the native state energy minimum to maintain structural homeostasis.
Seal the thermodynamic envelope defining the final macromolecular boundary.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Ribosomal translation machinery and chaperone proteins that decode genetic instructions into physical polymer chains.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Allosteric signal transduction and conformational wave propagation across the macromolecular surface.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: Covalent backbone sequence and epigenetic post translational modifications storing structural history.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: Thermodynamic driving forces and stochastic molecular collisions determining folding and binding kinetics.
phase_5.alfs_store: The universal genetic code and amino acid physicochemical property tables serving as the foundational reference.

phase_6.cycle_cost: Gibbs free energy consumed to overcome the entropic penalty of polymerization and conformational folding.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: The unactivated monomer pool existing in a high entropy disordered state within the bulk solvent.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: The crosslinked multimeric complex existing in a low entropy highly ordered native state.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0: The local entropy decrease of the macromolecule is exactly offset by the entropy increase of the released solvent molecules and hydrolyzed leaving groups.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → AFWD → CLINK → AFWD → CLINK → FSPLIT → EVALT → AFWD → EVALF → AREV → FFUSE → ENGAGR → IFIX → IMSCRIB → TANCH
-- Class: Macromolecule
-- Fingerprint: sig=(9,2,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=15
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(5, 10)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑾  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑼 → 𐑱  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [2] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑾 → 𐑾  | composition — regime coherence
--   [3] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑱 → 𐑱  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [4] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑾 → 𐑚  | composition — regime coherence
--   [5] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [6] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [7] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [8] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [9] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [10] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑳  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [11] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑙 → 𐑭  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [12] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑳 → 𐑠  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [13] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑭 → 𐑡  | identity — self-imscription
--   [14] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑠 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def macromolecule_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (Initialize the unactivated monomer pool in the aqueous solvent.)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑼 𐑱)  -- [1] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Activate the monomer by attaching a high energy leaving group.)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑾 𐑾)  -- [2] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Chain the activated monomer to the growing polymer backbone.)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑱 𐑱)  -- [3] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Propagate the polymer chain through sequential monomer addition.)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑾 𐑚)  -- [4] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Complete the primary sequence synthesis of the linear macromolecule.)
  -- FSPLIT [5] (gran := 𐑚) (Dissociate the multimeric complex into Monomeric subunit A and Monomeric subunit B.) / FFUSE [10] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (Fold Monomeric subunit A into the native functional conformation.)
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Expose the binding interface of Monomeric subunit A to the solvent.)
      -- F-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [8] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (Misfold Monomeric subunit B into an aggregated nonfunctional state.)
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [9] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (Bury the hydrophobic core of Monomeric subunit B via reverse conformational c...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [10]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑳)  -- [10] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Reassociate Monomeric subunit A and Monomeric subunit B to reconstitute the multimeric protein complex.)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑙 𐑭)  -- [11] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (Suspend the reconstituted complex in the molten globule phase holding both st...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑳 𐑠)  -- [12] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (Lock the quaternary structure via irreversible disulfide crosslinking.)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑭 𐑡)  -- [13] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Recognize the native state energy minimum to maintain structural homeostasis.)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑠 𐑼)  -- [14] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Seal the thermodynamic envelope defining the final macromolecular boundary.)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def macromolecule_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (macromolecule_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def macromolecule_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (macromolecule_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem macromolecule_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
